message:
feat(kernel): AA-02.3 decision-plane validation contract (35 tests)

Implement asupersync-1508v.2.6: deterministic validation for shadow,
canary, rollback, hold/release lifecycle, evidence-ledger completeness,
and bypass prevention for controller promotion pipeline.

Deliverables:
- docs/decision_plane_validation_contract.md
- artifacts/decision_plane_validation_v1.json (16 scenarios)
- scripts/run_decision_plane_validation_smoke.sh
- tests/decision_plane_validation_contract.rs (35 tests)

Also closes AA-02 epic (asupersync-1508v.2), unblocking AA-03 and AA-10.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -456,13 +456,13 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","title":"[AA-10.2] Implement CI gates for proof artifacts, calibration drift, and tail regressions","description":"Implement the hard gates that make the ascension program operationally real: proof/artifact consistency checks, calibration-drift alarms, tail-regression budgets, and rollout readiness validation.","design":"Scope:\n- Fail closed on missing or mismatched evidence.\n- Make calibration drift and tail regressions first-class CI failures.\n- Integrate with deterministic unit, E2E, and bench flows plus evidence bundles.","acceptance_criteria":"1. CI can validate artifact bundles, controller calibration state, track validation-pack coverage, composition eligibility, and tail-budget deltas.
2. Failures are actionable and point to the exact broken claim, artifact, workload, missing validation pack, or unsupported combination.
3. Deterministic unit, E2E, and bench commands plus structured logs are part of the gate, and failures emit exact rerun commands.
4. Progressive-delivery readiness is computed from explicit evidence, per-track coverage status, and composition status, not human memory.","notes":"Primary risk: noisy gates that people learn to ignore.\nCountermeasure: tie each gate to a concrete claim or invariant and tune against the AA-01 corpus.\nFallback: degraded or experimental tracks stay out of default rollout if evidence is insufficient.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:10.193652802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:42.878289387Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","ci","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:10.193652802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:53.033214189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:32:16.167470743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:55.533223182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:58.056030570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:00.574227580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:03.076018953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","title":"[AA-10.3] Produce comparative demos, closure packet, and launch doctrine for runtime ascension","description":"Produce the final closure packet for the ascension program: comparative demos, claim-backed benchmark summaries, known-risk register, rollback doctrine, and the operator/developer guidance needed to continue or ship the work responsibly.","design":"Scope:\n- Summarize what actually improved, what remained experimental, and why.\n- Include side-by-side comparisons against conservative baselines and any valid external references.\n- Leave behind a package future contributors can extend without reconstructing the original intent.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Closure packet links every important claim to evidence, unit tests, workloads, structured logs, rollback triggers, and cross-track compatibility verdicts.
2. Comparative demos and deterministic E2E drill scripts distinguish default-ready work from experimental work and explain the user-visible difference.
3. Known risks, non-goals, downgrade paths, unsupported combinations, and next-step decisions are explicit.
4. Final docs, runnable verification commands, composition-drill recipes, structured incident logs, and operator/developer troubleshooting recipes are self-contained enough to act as the continuation and incident-response point for future sessions.","notes":"Primary risk: turning closure into marketing instead of truth.\nCountermeasure: every headline result must cite the underlying evidence graph.\nFallback: if a track lacks closure-quality evidence, it remains explicitly experimental in the launch doctrine.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:15.209441938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:18:25.452896555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","release","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:15.209441938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:05.571178582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:37.874942303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:08.062936829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:10.619028203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:13.114106178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","title":"[AA-10.4] Run cross-track composition gauntlet, compatibility matrix, and incident drill pack","description":"Execute the full-system validation layer that the per-track beads do not cover on their own: deterministic composition tests across interacting advanced features, a compatibility matrix, and operator-grade incident drill artifacts.","design":"Scope:
- Compose the graduating tracks in the combinations that matter to real users: decision plane, control artifacts, fast-path substrate, static safety surfaces, trace intelligence, authority sandboxing, crash-only recovery, and transport experiments.
- Treat compatibility as a first-class artifact rather than an assumption hidden inside other validation beads.
- Build deterministic drills for incident handling, downgrade, rollback, and replay across track boundaries.
- Produce a matrix that distinguishes supported default combinations, supported-but-experimental combinations, and forbidden combinations.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E composition suites cover high-risk combinations across controllers, fast paths, static surfaces, trace intelligence, authority, recovery, and transport.
2. A compatibility matrix names supported, degraded, experimental, and forbidden combinations together with explicit fallback or downgrade behavior.
3. Structured logs, incident packets, and exact rerun commands let an operator reproduce any failing composition scenario directly.
4. Any cross-track regression in invariants, tail budgets, recovery behavior, authority containment, or diagnosability blocks closure.","notes":"Why this bead exists:
- Per-track validation is necessary but not sufficient. Users experience the composed system, not isolated tracks.
- The biggest late-stage risk in an ambitious runtime program is interaction failure: features that are individually correct but jointly unstable, opaque, or operationally painful.
- This bead is the plan-space answer to that failure mode.

Fallback doctrine:
- If a combination is not proven, it is explicitly marked unsupported or experimental.
- Default-ready closure requires both per-track green status and composition-level green status.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:10.178075830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:55.194329811Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","ci","release","runtime-ascension","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:10.178075830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:15.179026009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:17.692032263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:20.216484275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:22.736266190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:25.237995174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:27.734485164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:55.194257877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:30.257797392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:32.763947553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:35.339999483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.2","title":"[AA-02] Proof-Carrying Decision Plane Kernel","description":"Unify adaptive runtime logic behind one decision-plane kernel with explicit controller registration, artifact verification, evidence emission, rollout rules, and deterministic fallback semantics.","design":"Background:\n- The repository already contains decision-contract and adaptive-control ingredients, but they are still more like powerful islands than a single kernel.\n- This track turns controllers into first-class runtime artifacts rather than ad hoc code paths.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Runtime snapshot/registration contract for every controller.\n- Proof-carrying artifact format and verifier.\n- Policy registry with shadow/canary/ramp/rollback behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Controllers register against one explicit runtime snapshot contract with deterministic field semantics.
2. Controller artifacts carry assumptions, bounds, versioning, compatibility, signatures/hashes, and conservative fallback references.
3. Runtime loading rejects invalid or incompatible controller artifacts before they influence behavior.
4. Shadow mode, canary promotion, rollback, and evidence-ledger emission are standardized rather than reimplemented per subsystem.
5. The track emits deterministic unit and E2E validation paths, structured diagnostics, and exact rollback/rerun commands that make controller behavior inspectable for operators and future contributors.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: separate the deterministic data plane from an explicit, auditable decision plane.\n- EV: highest strategic leverage because it makes future radical controls safe to ship.\n- Primary risk: over-centralized complexity.\n- Countermeasure: narrow controller seam, typed snapshot contract, and fail-closed artifact verification.\n- Conservative fallback: current static config/runtime heuristics remain loadable and selectable as the default artifact.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:19.813234283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:56:54.841270637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","governance","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:19.813234283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:05.519270402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.2.1","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-02.1] Define RuntimeKernelSnapshot and controller registration contract","description":"Define the canonical runtime snapshot that controllers are allowed to observe and the registration contract every controller must satisfy before it can participate in decisions.","design":"Scope:\n- Specify snapshot fields, versioning, units, and update cadence.\n- Include mandatory controller metadata: decisions made, fallback active, calibration score, last action, artifact IDs, and budget counters.\n- Keep the seam narrow enough that the data plane remains deterministic and auditable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Snapshot fields are versioned, typed, and documented with ownership and update rules.\n2. Registration validation rejects controllers with missing metadata or unsupported field expectations.\n3. Unit tests cover snapshot serialization, compatibility checks, and deterministic update semantics.\n4. Structured logs expose snapshot/version IDs without leaking ambient authority or nondeterministic state.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.2.4 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.2.4.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:15.427183388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:08.459081115Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:08.458964047Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","formal","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:35.122774226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:15.427183388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.2.2","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-02.2] Implement proof-carrying controller artifact format and verifier","description":"Implement the artifact format that packages controller policy tables, assumptions, bounds, compatibility ranges, fallback references, and verification material into one deterministic load unit.","design":"Scope:\n- Define manifest schema, hash/signature chain, assumptions ledger, budget caps, and fallback pointer.\n- Implement a verifier that rejects mismatched, stale, or incompatible artifacts before runtime use.\n- Ensure artifact verification itself is deterministic and replayable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Controller artifacts include manifest, compatibility, bounds, assumptions, and fallback metadata.\n2. Loader/verifier rejects malformed or mismatched artifacts with deterministic diagnostics.\n3. Unit and E2E tests cover happy path, signature/hash mismatch, version mismatch, and fallback activation.\n4. Structured logs emit artifact IDs, verifier verdict, and rollback pointer.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.2.5 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.2.5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:20.405456193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:13.220187243Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:13.220073171Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:20.405456193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:37.626527356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.2.3","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-02.3] Validate shadow, canary, rollback, and evidence-ledger controller operations","description":"Create the explicit validation bead for decision-plane operations: shadow execution, canary gating, rollback drills, and evidence-ledger completeness for controller-driven runtime changes.","design":"Scope:\n- Exercise promote/hold/rollback flows for representative controllers.\n- Validate that fallback is instant, deterministic, and evidence-rich.\n- Ensure logs, artifacts, and operator-facing diagnostics are good enough for real incidents.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit tests cover state transitions for shadow, canary, hold, rollback, and fallback.\n2. Deterministic E2E scenarios prove controller promotion cannot bypass verification or evidence emission.\n3. Structured logs and artifacts record policy ID, decision reason, fallback trigger, and replay pointers.\n4. Failed rollouts produce direct, runnable recovery commands and leave the runtime in a conservative state.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.2.6 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.2.6.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:25.373909732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:17.932443315Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:17.932330415Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","runtime-ascension","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:25.373909732Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:40.106545858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","title":"[AA-02.1] Define RuntimeKernelSnapshot and controller registration contract","description":"Define the canonical runtime snapshot that controllers are allowed to observe and the registration contract every controller must satisfy before it can participate in decisions.","design":"Scope:\n- Specify snapshot fields, versioning, units, and update cadence.\n- Include mandatory controller metadata: decisions made, fallback active, calibration score, last action, artifact IDs, and budget counters.\n- Keep the seam narrow enough that the data plane remains deterministic and auditable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Snapshot fields are versioned, typed, and documented with ownership and update rules.
2. Registration validation rejects controllers with missing metadata or unsupported field expectations.
3. Deterministic unit and E2E tests cover snapshot serialization, compatibility checks, upgrade or downgrade behavior, and deterministic update semantics.
4. Structured logs expose snapshot IDs, version IDs, field provenance, and rejection reasons without leaking ambient authority or nondeterministic state.","notes":"Primary risk: overly broad snapshot surface.\nCountermeasure: start with a strict minimum and add fields only with explicit justification.\nFallback: controllers can consume a reduced snapshot and remain in shadow mode until richer fields exist.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:04.393495950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T16:05:10.653087402Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T16:05:10.653063357Z","close_reason":"Defined RuntimeKernelSnapshot/controller registration contract doc+artifact+smoke runner+invariants; validated via rch test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","formal","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:25.208239635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:04.393495950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Controller artifacts include manifest, compatibility, bounds, assumptions, and fallback metadata.\n2. Loader/verifier rejects malformed or mismatched artifacts with deterministic diagnostics.\n3. Unit and E2E tests cover happy path, signature/hash mismatch, version mismatch, and fallback activation.\n4. Structured logs emit artifact IDs, verifier verdict, and rollback pointer.","notes":"Primary risk: artifact format becomes too clever to audit.\nCountermeasure: keep manifests human-readable and verifiers fail-closed.\nFallback: continue loading only built-in static policies if artifact verification is unavailable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:09.371204836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T16:15:10.051256453Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T16:15:10.051230765Z","close_reason":"Implemented controller artifact format/verifier contract with deterministic cases, smoke runner, and invariant tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:09.371204836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:27.779807604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+      "description": "Recovery commands include actionable remediation steps for each rollback reason.",
+      "category": "recovery",

+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa023 cargo test --test decision_plane_validation_contract transition -- --nocapture"

@@ -0,0 +1,124 @@
+# Decision Plane Validation Contract
+
+Bead: `asupersync-1508v.2.6`
+
+## Purpose
+
+This contract defines deterministic validation scenarios for decision-plane controller operations: shadow execution, canary gating, promotion pipeline, rollback drills, hold/release lifecycle, and evidence-ledger completeness. It ensures that controller rollout cannot bypass verification gates and that failed rollouts produce actionable recovery commands.
+
+## Contract Artifacts
+
+1. Canonical artifact: `artifacts/decision_plane_validation_v1.json`
+2. Smoke runner: `scripts/run_decision_plane_validation_smoke.sh`
+3. Invariant suite: `tests/decision_plane_validation_contract.rs`
+
+## State Transition Model
+
+Controllers follow a strict promotion pipeline:
+
+```
+Shadow --> Canary --> Active
+  ^                    |
+  |   (rollback)       |
+  +--------------------+
+  ^         ^
+  |  Hold --+ (blocks promotion, release restores prior mode)
+  +--- Fallback (any rollback activates fallback flag)
+```
+
+Valid transitions:
+- `Shadow -> Canary` (requires calibration >= threshold AND epochs >= min_shadow_epochs)
+- `Canary -> Active` (requires calibration >= threshold AND epochs >= min_canary_epochs)
+- Any mode -> `Hold` (operator-initiated investigation pause)
+- `Hold -> (prior mode)` (release restores mode before hold)
+- `Canary/Active -> Shadow` (rollback on regression, budget, manual, or fallback)
+
+Invalid transitions:
+- `Shadow -> Active` (must pass through Canary)
+- `Hold -> (any promotion)` (must release first)
+
+## Rollback Contract
+
+Rollback always targets Shadow mode. Each rollback reason produces a `RecoveryCommand` with:
+
+1. Controller identity (ID, name)
+2. Mode transition (from, to)
+3. Rollback reason with structured payload
+4. Policy ID governing the decision
+5. Snapshot ID at time of rollback
+6. Actionable remediation steps
+
+Rollback of a controller already in Shadow is a no-op (returns `None`).
+
+## Evidence Ledger Contract
+
+Every state transition MUST produce an `EvidenceLedgerEntry` containing:
+
+- Sequential entry ID
+- Controller ID
+- Snapshot ID (when available)
+- Event type (Registered, Promoted, RolledBack, Held, Released, Deregistered, PromotionRejected, DecisionRecorded)
+- Policy ID
+- Timestamp
+
+Promotion rejections are also recorded, ensuring the ledger captures both successful and failed attempts.
+
+## Structured Logging Contract
+
+Decision-plane operations MUST emit structured logs including:
+
+- `controller_id`: Controller under operation
+- `controller_name`: Human-readable name
+- `mode`: Current controller mode
+- `previous_mode`: Mode before transition
+- `policy_id`: Promotion policy governing the operation
+- `calibration_score`: Current calibration score
+- `epochs_in_mode`: Epochs spent in current mode
+- `budget_overruns`: Accumulated budget overruns
+- `decision_label`: Label of the decision being recorded
+- `snapshot_id`: Snapshot ID for the operation
+- `verdict`: Outcome of the operation
+- `rejection_reason`: Why a promotion was rejected
+- `rollback_reason`: Why a rollback was triggered
+- `fallback_active`: Whether fallback is currently active
+- `recovery_command`: Recovery command payload (on rollback)
+- `ledger_entry_count`: Total ledger entries for this controller
+
+## Comparator-Smoke Runner
+
+Canonical runner: `scripts/run_decision_plane_validation_smoke.sh`
+
+The runner reads `artifacts/decision_plane_validation_v1.json` and emits:
+
+1. Per-scenario bundle manifests with schema `decision-plane-validation-smoke-bundle-v1`
+2. Aggregate run report with schema `decision-plane-validation-smoke-run-report-v1`
+
+Examples:
+
+```bash
+# List scenarios
+bash ./scripts/run_decision_plane_validation_smoke.sh --list
+
+# Dry-run one scenario
+bash ./scripts/run_decision_plane_validation_smoke.sh --scenario AA023-SMOKE-TRANSITIONS --dry-run
+
+# Execute one scenario
+bash ./scripts/run_decision_plane_validation_smoke.sh --scenario AA023-SMOKE-TRANSITIONS --execute
+```
+
+## Validation
+
+Focused invariant test command (routed through `rch`):
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa023 cargo test --test decision_plane_validation_contract -- --nocapture
+```
+
+## Cross-References
+
+- `artifacts/decision_plane_validation_v1.json`
+- `scripts/run_decision_plane_validation_smoke.sh`
+- `tests/decision_plane_validation_contract.rs`
+- `src/runtime/kernel.rs` -- ControllerRegistry, promotion pipeline, evidence ledger
+- `docs/controller_artifact_contract.md` -- AA-02.2 artifact format
+- `artifacts/controller_artifact_contract_v1.json`

@@ -0,0 +1,223 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Schema anchors for contract invariants:
+# - decision-plane-validation-smoke-bundle-v1
+# - decision-plane-validation-smoke-run-report-v1
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
+CONTRACT_ARTIFACT="${PROJECT_ROOT}/artifacts/decision_plane_validation_v1.json"
+OUTPUT_ROOT="${DECISION_PLANE_SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/decision-plane-validation-smoke}"
+TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"
+LIST_ONLY=0
+DRY_RUN=1
+
+declare -a SELECTED_SCENARIOS=()
+
+usage() {
+    cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: ./scripts/run_decision_plane_validation_smoke.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --list                    List scenario IDs and exit
+  --scenario <id>           Run one scenario (repeatable)
+  --output-root <dir>       Override output root
+  --dry-run                 Emit manifests without executing (default)
+  --execute                 Execute cargo test scenarios
+  -h, --help                Show help
+USAGE
+}
+
+require_tools() {
+    if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "FATAL: jq is required for decision plane validation smoke runner" >&2
+        exit 1
+    fi
+    if [ ! -f "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" ]; then
+        echo "FATAL: contract artifact missing at ${CONTRACT_ARTIFACT}" >&2
+        exit 1
+    fi
+}
+
+json_escape() {
+    printf '%s' "$1" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'
+}
+
+contract_version() {
+    jq -r '.contract_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+bundle_schema_version() {
+    jq -r '.runner_bundle_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+report_schema_version() {
+    jq -r '.runner_report_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+list_scenarios() {
+    jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[] | [.scenario_id, .description] | @tsv' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" \
+        | while IFS=$'\t' read -r sid desc; do
+            printf '%-38s %s\n' "$sid" "$desc"
+        done
+}
+
+load_scenario_json() {
+    local sid="$1"
+    jq -c --arg sid "$sid" '.smoke_scenarios[] | select(.scenario_id == $sid)' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+append_result() {
+    local entry="$1"
+    if [[ -z "${RESULTS_JSON:-}" ]]; then
+        RESULTS_JSON="$entry"
+    else
+        RESULTS_JSON="${RESULTS_JSON},${entry}"
+    fi
+}
+
+run_scenario() {
+    local sid="$1"
+    local scenario_json
+    scenario_json="$(load_scenario_json "$sid")"
+    if [[ -z "$scenario_json" ]]; then
+        echo "FATAL: unknown scenario id: ${sid}" >&2
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local description command
+    description="$(jq -r '.description' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    command="$(jq -r '.command' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+
+    local scenario_dir="${RUN_DIR}/${sid}"
+    local log_file="${scenario_dir}/run.log"
+    local summary_file="${scenario_dir}/bundle_manifest.json"
+    local started_ts ended_ts status rc
+
+    mkdir -p "$scenario_dir"
+    started_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+
+    echo ">>> Running scenario ${sid}"
+    echo "    description: ${description}"
+    echo "    command: ${command}"
+
+    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        printf 'DRY_RUN scenario=%s\n' "$sid" | tee "$log_file" >/dev/null
+        rc=0
+        status="dry_run"
+    else
+        rc=0
+        eval "$command" > "$log_file" 2>&1 || rc=$?
+        if [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]; then
+            status="passed"
+        else
+            status="failed"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    ended_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+
+    cat >"$summary_file" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(bundle_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "scenario_id": "$(json_escape "$sid")",
+  "description": "$(json_escape "$description")",
+  "command": "$(json_escape "$command")",
+  "artifact_path": "$(json_escape "$summary_file")",
+  "run_log_path": "$(json_escape "$log_file")",
+  "status": "$(json_escape "$status")",
+  "exit_code": ${rc},
+  "started_ts": "$(json_escape "$started_ts")",
+  "ended_ts": "$(json_escape "$ended_ts")"
+}
+JSON
+
+    append_result "$(jq -c '.' "$summary_file")"
+
+    [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --list)
+            LIST_ONLY=1
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --scenario)
+            SELECTED_SCENARIOS+=("${2:-}")
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        --output-root)
+            OUTPUT_ROOT="${2:-}"
+            RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"
+            shift 2
+            ;;
+        --dry-run)
+            DRY_RUN=1
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --execute)
+            DRY_RUN=0
+            shift
+            ;;
+        -h|--help)
+            usage
+            exit 0
+            ;;
+        *)
+            echo "Unknown argument: $1" >&2
+            usage >&2
+            exit 1
+            ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+require_tools
+
+if [[ "$LIST_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then
+    list_scenarios
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+if [[ "${#SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    mapfile -t SELECTED_SCENARIOS < <(jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[].scenario_id' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT")
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"
+RESULTS_JSON=""
+OVERALL_RC=0
+
+for sid in "${SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}"; do
+    if ! run_scenario "$sid"; then
+        OVERALL_RC=1
+    fi
+done
+
+RUN_REPORT="${RUN_DIR}/run_report.json"
+cat >"$RUN_REPORT" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(report_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "artifact_path": "$(json_escape "$RUN_REPORT")",
+  "run_dir": "$(json_escape "$RUN_DIR")",
+  "selected_scenarios": $(jq -nc --argjson ids "$(printf '%s\n' "${SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}" | jq -Rsc 'split("\n") | map(select(length > 0))')" '$ids'),
+  "dry_run": $( [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]] && printf 'true' || printf 'false' ),
+  "results": [${RESULTS_JSON}],
+  "status": "$([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "passed" || printf "failed")"
+}
+JSON
+
+echo ""
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "         DECISION PLANE VALIDATION SMOKE SUMMARY                   "
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "  Run dir:   ${RUN_DIR}"
+echo "  Report:    ${RUN_REPORT}"
+echo "  Mode:      $([ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ] && printf "DRY-RUN" || printf "EXECUTE")"
+echo "  Status:    $([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "PASSED" || printf "FAILED")"
+echo "==================================================================="
+
+exit "$OVERALL_RC"
